steps.title: Use the intercepts
steps.content: The graph is a budget line. The two easiest points to use are the intercepts: $(0,35)$ and $(90,0)$.

steps.title: Graph or test the choices
steps.content: In Desmos, graph the candidate equations and compare their intercepts. Choice B, $7x+18y=630$, passes through both intercepts: $7(0)+18(35)=630$ and $7(90)+18(0)=630$.

steps.title: Interpret the equation
steps.content: The equation $7x+18y=630$ means each T-shirt costs $7$, each sweatshirt costs $18$, and the total budget is $630$. That matches the graph's maximum of $90$ T-shirts or $35$ sweatshirts.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only choice B matches both graph intercepts and the fixed-budget context, so choice B is correct.
